name: Ermita de Nuestra Señora de los Desamparados – circular along the Guadiela River (PR-CU 47)
name_original: Ermita de Nuestra Señora de los Desamparados – circular por el río Guadiela (PR-CU 47)
region_intro: Easy hiking trails near Embalse de Buendía with parking offer access to a landscape shaped by the large reservoir and its surrounding natural areas. The region features gentle hills, Mediterranean scrubland, and varied terrain suitable for relaxed exploration. These trails provide opportunities to experience the local environment, often with views of the water and unique geological formations. The area is characterized by its tranquil atmosphere, making it ideal for those seeking accessible outdoor activities. Best easy hiking trails around Embalse de Buendía The most popular easy hiking route is Route of the Faces loop from Aparcamiento Ruta de Las Caras, a 1.0 mile (1.6 km) trail that takes 25 minutes to complete. This path features unique rock sculptures carved into the sandstone cliffs. Another top favourite among local hikers is Route of the Faces loop from PR-CU 46 Ruta de las Caras, an easy 1.5 miles (2.4 km) path. This route also explores the artistic rock formations, offering scenic views of the reservoir. Local hikers also love the Hiking loop from Sacedón, a 3.1 miles (5.0 km) trail leading through varied natural surroundings, often completed in about 1 hour 35 minutes. Hiking around Embalse de Buendía is defined by reservoir views, gentle hills, and unique artistic rock formations. The network offers options for different ability levels, with a focus on easy hiking trails. The routes in Embalse de Buendía are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 30 reviews. More than 300 hikers have used komoot to explore Embalse de Buendía's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many easy hiking trails are available around Embalse de Buendía?
A: There are 4 easy hiking trails specifically designed for relaxed exploration around Embalse de Buendía. In total, the region offers 18 hiking routes across various difficulty levels.

Q: Are there easy circular walks around Embalse de Buendía?
A: Yes, several easy circular walks are available. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31443085" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Route of the Faces loop from Aparcamiento Ruta de Las Caras&lt;/a&gt;, which is 1.0 mile (1.6 km) long and features unique rock sculptures. Another is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31477075" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hiking loop from Sacedón&lt;/a&gt;, a 3.1-mile (5.0 km) trail through varied natural surroundings.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the easy trails around Embalse de Buendía?
A: Most trails in the Embalse de Buendía area are dog-friendly, but it's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash, especially in areas with wildlife or near other hikers. Always check local regulations for specific routes.

Q: Are the easy trails suitable for families with children?
A: Yes, the easy trails around Embalse de Buendía are generally suitable for families. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31443085" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Route of the Faces loop from Aparcamiento Ruta de Las Caras&lt;/a&gt; are short and feature engaging rock sculptures, making them particularly enjoyable for children.

Q: What kind of views can I expect on these easy hikes?
A: The easy hikes offer diverse views, primarily featuring the expansive Embalse de Buendía reservoir, gentle hills, and Mediterranean scrubland. You'll also encounter unique artistic rock formations, especially along the Route of the Faces.

Q: Are there any historical sites or landmarks to see along the easy hiking trails?
A: While hiking, you can explore several interesting landmarks. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5440124" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ruins of La Isabela Spa&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5369092" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Royal Site of La Isabela&lt;/a&gt; are notable historical sites in the vicinity, offering a glimpse into the region's past.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Embalse de Buendía?
A: Spring and autumn are generally the best times for hiking around Embalse de Buendía. The weather is milder, and the natural scenery is vibrant. Summer can be hot, so early mornings or late afternoons are preferable, and winter offers a quieter experience, though some facilities might have reduced hours.

Q: Are there trails with shade for summer hiking in Embalse de Buendía?
A: While many trails offer open views of the reservoir, some sections may provide natural shade from trees and vegetation, particularly in areas away from the immediate shoreline. For longer summer hikes, it's advisable to start early or late in the day and carry plenty of water.

Q: Where can I find parking for the easy trails around Embalse de Buendía?
A: Many trailheads, especially for popular routes like the Route of the Faces, have designated parking areas. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31443085" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Route of the Faces loop&lt;/a&gt; starts from Aparcamiento Ruta de Las Caras, indicating available parking.

Q: Is public transport available to reach the trailheads?
A: Public transport options to directly access all trailheads around Embalse de Buendía can be limited. It is often more convenient to reach the area by car. However, some larger towns nearby may have bus services, from which you might need to arrange further transport to specific starting points.

Q: What do other hikers say about the easy trails in Embalse de Buendía?
A: The easy trails in Embalse de Buendía are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 30 reviews. Hikers often praise the tranquil atmosphere, the unique rock formations of the Route of the Faces, and the scenic views of the reservoir.

Q: Are there any viewpoints with easy access around Embalse de Buendía?
A: Yes, there are easily accessible viewpoints. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5398646" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peña de la Virgen Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; offers panoramic vistas of the reservoir and its surroundings, often reachable with minimal effort.

Q: What should I wear for hiking in Embalse de Buendía during spring?
A: In spring, wear layers as temperatures can vary. Comfortable hiking shoes are essential, along with breathable clothing. A light jacket or fleece is advisable for cooler mornings or evenings, and sun protection like a hat and sunscreen is always recommended.
tips:
About twelve kilometers from the town of Buendía and on the banks of the Guadiela River we find the Hermitage of Nuestra Señora de los Desamparados.
This place is also known as "El Sitio", since it was the place where the Virgin appeared in the 16th century. The surroundings of the hermitage are perfectly equipped with picnic areas, a drinking water fountain, numerous benches, even bathrooms, so that you can enjoy the spectacular scenery for as long as you want.

Located near the town in Cuenca that gives it its name, it was inaugurated in 1958 at a height of 78.10 m and with a water reservoir capacity of 1,638 hm³ in an area of 8,194 ha. Its waters bathe, among others, the towns of Buendía, Alcocer, Alcohujate, Villalba del Rey. From it, you can access the route of the Hermitage of the Virgen de los Desamparados 'Favorite Site', which is worth visiting.

waypoints.name: Hermitage of Our Lady of the Forsaken
